connection.dualTip: WebGUI API + SSH connected, all features available
connection.apiTip: WebGUI API only, terminal and file manager unavailable

nav.dashboardHint: Server CPU, memory, network and disk overview
nav.storageHint: Array & cache disk capacity, temperature, health
nav.terminalHint: SSH command line in your browser

sidebar.confirmDeleteDesc: This will disconnect and remove saved configuration. Are you sure?

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.cpuNoData: This CPU does not report usage/temperature
dashboard.cannotFetch: Unable to fetch data. Make sure the backend is connected to Unraid.

docker.title: Docker Containers
docker.confirmStopDesc: Are you sure you want to {{action}} container "{{name}}"? Running services will be interrupted.

vms.title: Virtual Machines
vms.cannotFetch: Unable to fetch VM info. Make sure libvirt/libvirtd is enabled on Unraid.
vms.confirmForceStopDesc: Force stopping VM "{{name}}" may cause data loss. Consider using "Graceful Shutdown" first. Continue?
vms.vncDesc: Connect to the VM's VNC service via SSH tunnel. If no display appears, ensure the VM has a VNC graphics device configured.

storage.title: Storage Array
storage.confirmStopArrayDesc: All disks will be unmounted. Are you sure?
storage.confirmCorrectingDesc: Correcting check will auto-fix inconsistent parity data and may take hours. Continue?
storage.confirmCheckDesc: This will read all array disks and may take hours. Continue?
storage.smartFailDetail: Self-test failed — This disk may be failing. Back up data immediately!
storage.cannotFetch: Unable to fetch disk info. Make sure the backend is connected to Unraid.

files.cannotReadDir: Cannot read directory. Make sure SFTP is ready.
files.name: Name
files.confirmDeleteDesc: Delete {{count}} file(s)? This cannot be undone.
files.cannotPreview: Cannot preview this file type. Please download it instead.
files.fileTooLarge: File is large, showing first 64KB only.
files.unsavedDesc: You have unsaved changes. Discard them?

terminal.title: SSH Terminal
terminal.desc: Access Unraid command line directly in browser · Multiple sessions
terminal.tip: Sessions are forwarded via backend SSH. Click reconnect after disconnection.
terminal.wsError: [WebSocket error — backend terminal service not ready]

onboarding.selectKeyFile: Select or paste private key file
onboarding.title: unraid-plus Setup
onboarding.subtitle: Connect your Unraid in three minutes
onboarding.openSource: Apache 2.0 · Fully open source · Passwords only exist briefly in backend memory
onboarding.feature2Desc: Direct server command line in browser, no client needed
onboarding.feature3Desc: No cloud services, traffic stays on your LAN
onboarding.feature4Desc: All terms explained, easy for first-time users
onboarding.welcomeDesc1: A more intuitive manager than the official WebUI, easier to deploy than mobile apps.
onboarding.welcomeDesc2: Just your Unraid LAN IP and root password — connect in three minutes.
onboarding.beginnerDesc: Just set up Unraid, not sure what SSH is
onboarding.casualDesc: Used Docker and CLI, but don't want to fiddle with configs
onboarding.expertDesc: Know Linux well, give me the fastest setup path
onboarding.familiarityDesc: We'll adjust hint detail accordingly — not too wordy, not missing key info.
onboarding.connectDesc: Enter your Unraid WebGUI address and password. SSH will be configured automatically.
onboarding.serverAddressHint: The address you use in the browser for Unraid, e.g. tower.local or 192.168.1.99
onboarding.passwordHint: The password you use to log into the Unraid web interface (also the SSH password by default).
onboarding.nicknameHint: A memorable name, e.g. "Living Room NAS" or "Tower".
onboarding.advancedSettings: Advanced Settings (SSH port, username, key auth)
onboarding.usernameHint: Default root, usually no need to change.
onboarding.sshHostHint: Only needed if Unraid address differs from SSH address, e.g. reverse proxy.
onboarding.sshHostPlaceholder: Leave empty to auto-derive from server address
onboarding.sshKeyHint: Select your private key file, or paste PEM content directly.
onboarding.securityNote: Password is only used briefly in backend memory and never written to disk. Both WebGUI API and SSH channels are established on success.
onboarding.verifyDesc: Confirm the connection info below matches your Unraid server to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.
onboarding.sshNotConnected: SSH not connected — Terminal and file manager unavailable. Check SSH config in Settings.
onboarding.fingerprintNote: On first connection the server fingerprint is remembered. If it changes later, unraid-plus will refuse the connection and prompt you to verify.
onboarding.passwordMinLen: Password must be at least 4 characters
onboarding.securityDesc: Set a password for the unraid-plus web interface to prevent unauthorized access on your LAN.
onboarding.passwordFromEnv: Password is configured via environment variable, no need to set again.
onboarding.noPasswordWarning: No password = anyone on your LAN can access unraid-plus, including running SSH commands and restarting containers.
onboarding.uiPasswordHint: Leave empty to skip (can set later in Settings). At least 4 characters.
onboarding.doneDesc: Connected. Head to the dashboard to see your server status.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Connection, security & UI preferences
settings.currentConnectionDesc: The server this unraid-plus instance is managing
settings.keyFreeNote: After switching to key-pair mode, the root password is no longer used.
settings.keyFreeDesc: Backend auto-generates and manages SSH key pair. Root password never leaves memory.
settings.passwordEnabledDesc: Access password set. Unauthenticated users cannot operate.
settings.passwordDisabledDesc: Not enabled. Anyone can access this interface. Consider setting a password.
settings.onboardingDoneDesc: Turning off will force the welcome wizard to show again.
settings.autoRefreshDesc: Affects dashboard, Docker, storage and other live data.
settings.confirmDisconnectDesc: Disconnect from current server? You'll need to go through the connection wizard again.
settings.rotateKeyDesc: Generate a new key pair and deploy to Unraid? This replaces the existing authorized_keys on the server.
settings.passwordMinLen: Password must be at least 4 characters
